prompt:
Our onboarding scavenger hunts run on little text adventures and I'm tired of hand-coding each one — I want ONE engine that loads any world from a JSON file, so the content folks can ship new hunts without touching Python. Build adventure.py, stdlib only. Two very different worlds (world1.json, world2.json) ship with the acceptance tests and both must run on the same engine.

World file shape: top level has "start" (a room id), "victory" ({"room": id, "item": name}) and "rooms". Each room has "name", "desc", optional "exits" (direction -> room id), optional "items" (list of names, may be multi-word like 'brass key'), and optional "locked" (direction -> the exact item name that opens it; the lock sits on the room you're leaving). `load(path)` reads a file; `Game(world_dict)` takes the parsed dict. Reject broken worlds with ValueError: missing/unknown start room, an exit pointing at a room that doesn't exist, a locked direction that isn't an exit of that room, a victory room that doesn't exist or a victory entry without an item, any room missing name or desc.

Game exposes `location`, `inventory` (in pick-up order), `won`, `look()` and `execute(command)` which returns the reply text. Look output, exactly: the room name line, the desc line, then 'items: a, b' (omitted when the room is empty) in room order, then 'exits: ...' (omitted if none) with directions sorted alphabetically and locked ones suffixed ' (locked)'.

Verbs (first word; the rest of the line is the argument): `go DIR` — unknown way: "You can't go that way."; locked: "The DIR door is locked."; success returns the new room's look. `look` and `inventory` ("You carry: a, b" / "You carry nothing.") take no argument. `take X` / "There is no X here."; `drop X` / "You don't have the X." — success replies "You take the X." / "You drop the X.". `unlock DIR`: no such exit -> "There is no door to the DIR."; not locked -> "The DIR door isn't locked."; locked and you hold the right item -> "You unlock the DIR door." (permanent); otherwise "You don't have the key for that.". `use X`: not carried -> "You don't have the X."; carried and this room has a locked exit whose key is X -> unlock the alphabetically first such direction and reply "You use the X. The DIR door unlocks."; carried but useless here -> "Nothing happens.". Anything else: "I don't understand."

After EVERY command, if the player is in the victory room holding the victory item, append '\nYou win!' to the reply once and set won — that includes winning by taking the item inside the room or by walking in already carrying it.

CLI: `python3 adventure.py WORLD.json` prints the starting room's look, then for each stdin line prints '> ' + the stripped command followed by the reply; stop after a win (rest of stdin ignored) or at EOF, exit 0.

test_adventure.py plus the two world files are the acceptance contract — python3 test_adventure.py must pass, none of them get edited.
